stories.headline: Trump cancels planned Iran strikes, claims war 'settled'; says a memorandum could be signed within days
stories.summary: President Trump abruptly canceled airstrikes he had threatened hours earlier and said the US had reached 'a great settlement of the war with Iran,' predicting a 'conceptual' memorandum could be signed in Europe within days. He said talks had reached 'the highest level of Iranian leadership.' Tehran has not confirmed any deal, Israel says it is not a party, and the reversal followed a second day of mutual strikes and Iranian attacks on US Gulf bases.

perspectives.keyFraming: Whiplash diplomacy: Trump went from threatening to seize Iran's oil hub to declaring the war settled within hours, with no Iranian confirmation.
perspectives.summary: Trump said he canceled the scheduled strikes because discussions had been 'brought to the highest level of Iranian leadership and approved,' and that a 'very strong memorandum of understanding that is a little conceptual' could be signed 'maybe in Europe' within days. He claimed the Strait of Hormuz would reopen and the naval blockade lift on signing, while Iran had not confirmed any terms.

sources.title: Trump now says a peace deal will be announced 'soon,' cancels further strikes
Iran live updates: Trump says agreement with Iran could be signed as early as this weekend

perspectives.keyFraming: European and Gulf outlets cast the reversal as pressure-driven, coming after fresh Iranian attacks on US bases rather than a clear US victory.
perspectives.summary: The Washington Post reported Trump withdrew his threat to seize Iran's Kharg Island oil terminal after Iran struck up to five US bases, with verified Iranian imagery showing damage to 228 or more structures. Coverage framed the about-face as a de-escalation forced by the costs of escalation, while cautioning that earlier 2026 ceasefires had collapsed.

sources.title: Trump says he has canceled plans to strike Iran, claiming deal is near
Live updates: Trump says US 'ended the war with Iran,' though Tehran has yet to confirm a deal

perspectives.keyFraming: Trump and allies cast the settlement as Iran capitulating after a sustained US 'pounding,' though GOP hawks demand congressional review and removal of Iran's enriched uranium.
perspectives.summary: Fox News reported Trump canceled the Thursday-evening strikes after talks reached Iran's top leadership, saying Iran agreed never to obtain a nuclear weapon — the campaign's central aim. Asked why now, he said 'because they've taken a pounding.' Sen. Lindsey Graham urged that any deal be 'presented to Congress for review and approval' and that Iran's highly enriched uranium be removed.

sources.title: Trump says he's canceled Iran strikes, adds potential deal-signing 'to be announced shortly'
The Memo: Trump announces 'great settlement' on Iran, but the world awaits details

stories.headline: Oil tumbles about 3% as Trump cancels Iran strikes and claims the war is 'settled' pending a signing
stories.summary: Crude reversed an earlier war-premium spike on June 11 after President Trump abruptly canceled strikes he had threatened hours earlier and said the US had 'made a great settlement of the war with Iran,' subject to finalization. Brent settled near $90.4 (down about 3%) and WTI near $87.7, both extending losses after-hours. Iran did not confirm a deal, and its claimed total closure of the Strait of Hormuz remained disputed by US CENTCOM, leaving a residual war premium.

perspectives.keyFraming: Traders faded the morning spike on the strike cancellation while keeping a war premium given no confirmed Iranian sign-off.
perspectives.summary: Brent swung below $93 intraday before settling down about 3% near $90.38; WTI fell about 2% to $87.71. Trump's cancellation of planned strikes and claim of a settlement 'subject to finalization' drove the reversal. Reports said tanker traffic through Hormuz had increased, easing acute supply fears even as Iran's IRGC claimed a total closure that CENTCOM disputed.

sources.title: Brent, WTI oil prices: Trump calls off Iran strikes
Trump claims Iran war settled 'subject to finalization,' expects signing in 'next few days'

perspectives.keyFraming: A durable deal reopening Hormuz could unwind the oil shock behind the worst inflation in three years — a potential economic win.
perspectives.summary: Bloomberg reported Trump said discussions had been 'brought to the highest level of Iranian leadership and approved.' Analysts noted a credible deal would restore disrupted Gulf flows and relieve pump prices into the FOMC, while skeptics flagged Iran's silence and Trump's repeated same-day reversals as reasons for caution.

sources.title: Trump Cancels Planned Strikes Against Iran in Latest Reversal

perspectives.keyFraming: Another same-day reversal underscores erratic policymaking; Iran has not confirmed any deal and the strait remains contested.
perspectives.summary: NPR and the Washington Post noted Trump went from vowing to 'hit 'em hard tonight' to canceling strikes and announcing a settlement within hours, with no Iranian confirmation and US bases freshly targeted, keeping the risk of a return to all-out war elevated even as markets chose to price in de-escalation.

sources.title: Trump now says a peace deal will be announced 'soon,' cancels further strikes
Trump says he has canceled plans to strike Iran, claiming deal is near

stories.headline: Dow closes below 50,000 as the AI-chip selloff deepens; Broadcom and Nvidia lead losses
stories.summary: US stocks closed lower June 11 as the semiconductor selloff stretched into a third day. The Dow fell 1.9% to about 49,918, its first close below 50,000 since mid-May; the Nasdaq slid about 2% and the S&P 500 lost about 1.6%. Broadcom (-5.1%) and Nvidia (-3.7%) led declines after Broadcom failed to raise its AI-chip outlook; Micron, Intel and AMD fell about 6%. Sticky 4.2% CPI and a hawkish Fed setup into the June 16-17 FOMC compounded the risk-off tone.

perspectives.keyFraming: The AI trade is correcting as a weak Broadcom outlook collides with rate-hike fears, dragging megacap chips and the broad indexes lower.
perspectives.summary: Broadcom's failure to lift its AI-chip guidance triggered a multi-day chip rout; on June 11 Nvidia fell 3.7% and Broadcom 5.1%, with Micron, Intel and AMD down about 6%. The Dow closed below 50,000 for the first time since mid-May as investors repriced the AI trade against a 4.2% CPI print and rising Fed-hike odds into the June 16-17 meeting.

sources.title: Stock market today: Nasdaq plunges as AI trade halts on Fed hike bets

perspectives.keyFraming: Concentrated AI and megacap exposure leaves the indexes vulnerable, with a single guidance miss wiping out weeks of gains.
perspectives.summary: CNN noted the market whipsawed as the AI selloff resumed, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq suffering some of their worst days of the year and chip-stock rebounds repeatedly failing. The breadth problem — gains driven by a handful of AI names — amplifies the downside when sentiment on the trade sours.

sources.title: Markets whipsaw as AI sell-off resumes

stories.headline: Trump's Iran 'settlement' claim collides with Senate war-powers push and bipartisan demands for review
stories.summary: Trump's announcement that he had 'settled' the Iran war landed as the Senate pressed a war-powers resolution and lawmakers in both parties demanded any deal be submitted to Congress. Trump argued the war-powers clock no longer applies because hostilities have 'terminated,' while GOP hawks insisted on reviewing the terms and removing Iran's enriched uranium, setting up a constitutional clash even if the unsigned deal holds.

perspectives.keyFraming: The administration says the war-powers question is moot because hostilities have 'terminated,' setting up a procedural clash even if a deal holds.
perspectives.summary: PBS reported Trump claimed the congressional deadline to authorize the war 'doesn't apply' because hostilities had ended. Analysts note that even a passed resolution faces a likely veto, and that a fragile, unsigned settlement — with Tehran disputing the terms — leaves the underlying authorization question unresolved.

sources.title: Trump says deadline for Congress to approve Iran war doesn't apply, claiming hostilities have 'terminated'

perspectives.keyFraming: Hawks want the deal scrutinized, not the war restrained, demanding congressional review and removal of Iran's highly enriched uranium.
perspectives.summary: Fox News reported Sen. Lindsey Graham warned the emerging deal had 'troubling aspects' and should be 'presented to Congress for review and approval,' echoing the Obama-era review model and insisting Iran's enriched uranium be controlled and removed before any sanctions relief.

sources.title: Top GOP hawk Graham warns Iran deal has 'troubling aspects' as ceasefire begins

perspectives.keyFraming: Democrats want a binding vote regardless of Trump's unilateral 'settlement' claims, calling the conflict an unconstitutional war of choice.
perspectives.summary: NPR reported Sen. Tim Kaine and Leader Schumer continued pressing for a final Senate vote on the war-powers resolution, arguing a presidential ceasefire claim is no substitute for congressional authorization and that Congress must reassert its war powers even as the administration declares the fighting over.

sources.title: Trump now says a peace deal will be announced 'soon,' cancels further strikes
